jokes.text: Michael dramatically calling for help because he hurt his foot, treating it like a life-threatening emergency
jokes.setup: Michael calls office sounding like he's dying
jokes.punchline: It's just a burned foot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott character comedy - his complete lack of perspective on injury severity. The buildup with Pam thinking he's dying makes the reveal funnier.

jokes.text: Michael's elaborate George Foreman Grill breakfast-in-bed system explanation
jokes.setup: Michael needs to explain how he burned his foot
jokes.punchline: Overly complex system involving sleeping with a grill by his bed
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd solution to wanting breakfast in bed. The matter-of-fact delivery and defensive 'Sue me' makes it funnier. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: The grill 'clamped down' on Michael's foot
jokes.setup: How exactly did the injury happen?
jokes.punchline: He describes the grill like it attacked him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy description. Michael's personification of the grill as aggressive fits his victim mentality.

jokes.text: Michael's pronunciation of 'protuberance' as 'protruberence'
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces the word completely
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism. Shows his insecurity about appearing smart while failing at it.

jokes.text: Michael admits his weekend girlfriend was 'all made up'
jokes.punchline: Forced to admit he lied about having one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael cringe moment - his lie unraveling when he needs actual help. Shows his pathetic desperation.

jokes.text: Dwight crashes his car into a pole while rushing to save Michael
jokes.setup: Dwight dramatically rushing to Michael's rescue
jokes.punchline: Immediately crashes, making things worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Dwight's over-the-top devotion leads to actual injury worse than Michael's. Visual slapstick with character payoff.

jokes.text: 'You forgot your bumper!' shouted to Dwight as he drives away
jokes.setup: Dwight just crashed into a pole
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about the aftermath
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - treating major car damage as a minor inconvenience. The casual delivery works.

jokes.text: Michael's dramatic entrance demanding not to be freaked out about while clearly seeking attention
jokes.setup: Michael says don't freak out, treat him normally
jokes.punchline: Everything about his behavior screams 'pay attention to me'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael contradiction - says one thing while desperately wanting the opposite. The self-awareness mixed with complete lack of awareness.

jokes.text: Pam correcting 'cooked your foot' to 'burned my foot' with Michael's indignant response
jokes.punchline: Michael's wounded correction shows his sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay setup. Michael's defensive reaction to the 'wrong' cooking verb shows his absurd priorities.

jokes.text: Kevin's 'So where are you shipping your foot?' followed by 'Your foot' when no one laughs
jokes.setup: Kevin makes a pun about Michael's bandaged foot
jokes.punchline: Repeats the punchline when it bombs, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Kevin's social awkwardness on display. The failed pun followed by repeating it is perfectly in character and cringe.

jokes.text: Michael's job description for Pam: 'Your job is being my friend'
jokes.setup: Pam points out she was doing her actual job
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals his warped view of employee relationships
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael line showing his complete misunderstanding of professional relationships. Highly quotable and revealing.

jokes.text: Michael wanting family member treatment for 'serious physical trauma' vs Pam offering aspirin for being 'fussy'
jokes.punchline: Pam treats him like a cranky child
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's drama and Pam's practical response. 'Fussy' perfectly deflates his martyrdom.

jokes.text: Michael's 'bloody stump of a foot' description
jokes.punchline: Dramatically exaggerated medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to catastrophize. 'Bloody stump' is absurdly over the top for a minor burn.

jokes.text: Dwight cutting off mid-sentence about his duties, creating awkward silence
jokes.setup: Dwight starts explaining his duties to care for Michael
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's revealing too much devotion and stops
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Dwight catching himself revealing his obsessive devotion. The silence is the punchline.

jokes.text: Dwight's elaborate meal preparation - three whole chickens for dark meat, no yams story
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-the-top accommodation and suspicious yam shortage
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's excessive devotion to Michael's needs. The yam investigation adds absurd detail.

jokes.text: Jim's Russian music download site prank on Pam
jokes.setup: Jim offers to help with music downloads
jokes.punchline: Claims all songs are in Russian, then reveals he's kidding
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank setup with good timing on the reveal. Shows his playful relationship with Pam.

jokes.text: Pam's confused 'Why would they all be...' after Jim's prank
jokes.punchline: Pam's genuine confusion about the logic
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Pam actually considering the fake premise. Her innocence makes it work.

jokes.text: Jim calling Pam 'Pan' and her confused response
jokes.setup: Jim says goodbye using wrong name
jokes.punchline: Pam's delayed, confused 'Pan?'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Pam's delayed reaction and confusion work well. Shows Jim's casual mischief.

jokes.text: Michael asking Pam to rub butter (Country Crock) on his foot
jokes.punchline: Suggests butter, specifically brand-name margarine
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Michael request. The specific brand name makes it funnier and more pathetic.

jokes.text: Dwight's 'indestructible' cover demonstration that immediately breaks
jokes.setup: Dwight claims covers are totally indestructible
jokes.punchline: It breaks immediately when thrown
jokes.explanation: Classic irony setup with immediate payoff. Dwight's confidence makes the failure funnier.

jokes.text: Dwight pretending it didn't break and continuing the sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Dwight acts like nothing happened and doubles down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight behavior - never admitting failure even when obvious. The persistence makes it cringier.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head about Pam and Dwight being friends
jokes.punchline: His bemused reaction to the friendship
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke. Jim's confusion is relatable but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Pam's talking head progression from denial to horror about being Dwight's friend
jokes.punchline: Realizes with horror that she actually is his friend
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from denial to dawning horror. Shows character growth in unexpected direction.

jokes.text: Ryan's terrible pickup line 'you're sweeter than candy'
jokes.punchline: Delivers awful, generic pickup line
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor. Ryan's try-hard nature comes through but the line itself is predictably bad.

jokes.text: Michael falling off toilet and getting stuck between toilet and wall
jokes.setup: Michael's foot injury affecting basic functions
jokes.punchline: Creates an even more humiliating situation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation. Michael's dignity continues to erode in increasingly pathetic ways.

jokes.text: Michael requesting Ryan to 'clean me up a little bit' while stuck
jokes.punchline: Implies he needs to be cleaned, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Adds bathroom humor element to physical comedy. Michael's complete loss of dignity.

jokes.text: Jim claiming Ryan is dead to avoid helping Michael
jokes.punchline: Jim's completely casual lie about death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan absurdity. Jim's willingness to claim death to avoid Michael shows perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Michael's 'you only grilled your foot' dismissal from Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual dismissal using cooking terminology
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier cooking terminology. Jim's casual cruelty and word choice work well together.

jokes.text: Michael's disability lecture dismissing Phyllis's scoliosis as 'woman's trouble'
jokes.setup: Michael asking who knows about disability
jokes.punchline: Dismisses real condition with sexist comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael insensitivity. Shows his self-centeredness and casual sexism while claiming victim status.

jokes.text: Creed's iron lung story and age confusion
jokes.punchline: Creed claims outdated medical device, age becomes unclear
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed weirdness. The iron lung reference raises questions about his actual age in typical Creed fashion.

jokes.text: Michael's comment about Stanley having 'his fair share of obstacles'
jokes.punchline: Implies Stanley faces obstacles due to race
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate racial comment. Shows his obliviousness to his own insensitivity.

jokes.text: Stanley's 'Mail Boxes, Etc.' response to what Michael's bandage looks like
jokes.setup: Michael asking what his injury looks like
jokes.punchline: Stanley's completely unimpressed, practical observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line - completely deflates Michael's drama with mundane observation. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: The Stevie Wonder bathroom hypothetical and response 'We love Stevie Wonder'
jokes.punchline: Oscar's sincere response missing the point
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Michael analogy with innocent Oscar response. The sincerity of 'we love Stevie Wonder' works.

jokes.text: Michael's dramatic 'I burned my foot!' outburst
jokes.punchline: Explosive repetition of his minor injury
jokes.explanation: Good emotional escalation moment. Michael's childish outburst shows his frustration at not getting sympathy.

jokes.text: Tom Hanks disability analysis - Forrest Gump, Philadelphia, and Big confusion
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his disability poster choices
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous justifications including growing overnight as disability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic from Michael. Classifying rapid aging as disability and Jim's corrections make this memorable.

jokes.text: Stanley doing crossword puzzle during disability presentation
jokes.punchline: Stanley completely ignoring it for crossword
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley behavior - complete disinterest in Michael's dramatics. Visual gag that shows character.

jokes.text: Billy the wheelchair user's practical responses vs Michael's assumptions
jokes.punchline: Billy's matter-of-fact, unimpressed responses
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's stereotypes and Billy's reality. Billy's deadpan responses deflate Michael's drama.

jokes.text: Tooth brushing taking 'three times as long' mishearing
jokes.setup: Michael asking about daily tasks taking longer
jokes.punchline: Billy's normal time somehow becomes evidence of being three times slower
jokes.explanation: Michael's bad math and assumptions. Shows his desire to create problems that don't exist.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head: 'I want to clamp Michael's face in a George Foreman Grill'
jokes.punchline: Poetic justice using the same device that started everything
jokes.explanation: Exceptional callback and escalation. Perfect poetic justice and highly quotable. Jim's rare moment of expressed violence is cathartic.

jokes.text: Born on the Fourth of July reference and Michael's disappointment in Billy
jokes.punchline: Disappointed Billy doesn't match movie stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's reliance on media stereotypes. His disappointment in real person not matching movies is perfect.

jokes.text: Describing Dwight as 'moon-faced kid who crashed into the pole'
jokes.punchline: Casual cruel description of Dwight's accident
jokes.explanation: 'Moon-faced' is cruel but funny descriptor. Shows Michael's casual dismissal of Dwight's injury while obsessing over his own.

jokes.text: Ryan's aspirin-in-pudding trick compared to dog medicine
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining how he helped Michael
jokes.punchline: Compares Michael to his dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that perfectly captures how to handle Michael. Ryan's practical solution with insulting comparison works well.

jokes.text: Michael's 'blood coursing through my foot veins' recovery speech
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic medical language about minor improvement
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to overdramatize. 'Foot veins' is oddly specific anatomical nonsense.

jokes.text: Jim's shotgun rule explanation while Dwight has a concussion
jokes.setup: Question about seating arrangement for injured person
jokes.punchline: Jim's rigid adherence to arbitrary rules despite circumstances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - following rules regardless of context. The formality of his explanation contrasts well with the absurd situation.

jokes.text: Michael's judgment of Meredith's minivan affecting her dating prospects
jokes.punchline: Michael's shallow analysis of her relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael superficial judgment. Shows his priorities and assumptions about women.

jokes.text: Dwight drinking mysterious liquid from under van seat
jokes.punchline: Drinks unknown substance found under car seat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's concussion symptoms. Gross-out humor with character logic.

jokes.text: Michael claiming Dwight can't be fired because 'I don't work in this van'
jokes.setup: Michael threatening to fire Dwight for not giving bottle
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confused logic about employment jurisdiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant confused logic from concussed Dwight. The geographical limitation on employment is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: Dwight's middle name being 'Danger' vs 'Kurt'
jokes.setup: Jim asking for Dwight's middle name for hospital forms
jokes.punchline: Dwight claims 'Danger' but Jim knows it's actually Kurt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's desire to seem cool vs reality. Jim's knowledge of the truth adds depth.

jokes.text: Michael's 'miraculous recovery' vs Dwight needing CAT scan comparison
jokes.setup: Both have injuries, going to hospital
jokes.punchline: Michael claims he's better while Dwight needs serious medical attention
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's self-centeredness - claiming miracle while ignoring Dwight's serious injury.

jokes.text: Michael's 'That's what she said' theft from Dwight
jokes.setup: Doctor's medical statement sets up the joke
jokes.punchline: Michael steals Dwight's joke and claims ownership
jokes.explanation: Standard 'That's what she said' joke elevated by Michael's petty theft of it from injured Dwight.

jokes.text: Jim and Pam's phone conversation about Dwight and Michael
jokes.punchline: Jim's surprised reaction to her concern
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Pam/Dwight friendship theme. Shows character development but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Doctor telling Michael to 'stop that' during breathing exercise
jokes.punchline: Michael doing something inappropriate requiring correction
jokes.explanation: Implies Michael is doing something wrong even during simple medical procedure. Fits character but not exceptional.
